questions.text: I prefer to dive into a project and let the structure emerge organically.
I routinely make decisions based on what will best serve my personal trajectory.
I see myself as a valued crew member of whatever ship I am on.
I feel that I respond to the world with a kind of natural equanimity.
I am attuned to the way people signal closeness or distance through physical proximity.
I feel most confident when I have rehearsed or prepared thoroughly for an event.
I hold the view that treating another's pain as genuinely important is not a sentiment but an action that requires discipline.
I think that the ability to engage with suffering without emotional destabilization is a form of resilience that serves both the helper and the helped.
I hold the view that the moral concern generated by suffering is the inner compass that most reliably points me toward the right action in ambiguous circumstances.
I find it satisfying to override inertia and get things moving.
I find that personal success feels hollow unless it also lifts up the people around me.
I find that I pay more attention to what is said than to the unspoken dynamics in a conversation.
I tend to feel a surge of intense emotion before I have any cognitive understanding of what triggered it.
I find that establishing clear procedures prevents mistakes and miscommunication.
I enjoy being the public face of a group effort.
I tend to measure my worth by how much I've contributed to others, not by what I've accumulated.
I notice when people speak differently depending on who else is listening.
I find that my emotional sensitivity means I need more downtime than most people.
